sanskrit:
अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च।
न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते॥
transliteration:
ahaṃ hi sarva-yajñānāṃ bhoktā ca prabhur eva ca |
na tu mām abhijānanti tattvenātaś cyavanti te ||
literal_meaning: I (aham) indeed (hi) of all sacrifices (sarva-yajñānāṃ) the enjoyer (bhoktā) and (ca) the Lord (prabhuḥ) only (eva) and (ca); not (na) but (tu) Me (mām) they know (abhijānanti) in reality (tattvena), therefore (ataḥ) they fall (cyavanti) they (te).
contextual_meaning: All human striving—rituals, goals, relationships—ultimately seeks a single psychological ‘center’ (Krishna). Misidentifying this center with partial objects (status, people, outcomes) splits the psyche; re-integration requires recognizing the true source of satisfaction.
psychological_state: Fragmented self-structure chasing external validators; potential for cohesion once the archetypal ‘enjoyer’ within is acknowledged.
trigger_scenario: Working 70-hour weeks to afford a lifestyle that still feels hollow—realizing the promotion, house, or likes are placeholders for an unnamed inner presence.
anchor_text: Every act is for the One; know the One and stay whole.
